name: Cabanes and Via Augusta Loop
name_original: Desde Benlloc hasta Vilafames en tour circular por la Vía Augusta
description: Cycle the Cabanes and Via Augusta Loop, a 22.3-mile route through Roman history, orange groves, and charming villages in Castellón.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Benlloch offer diverse landscapes, from flat sections to demanding climbs, catering to various skill levels. The region is characterized by picturesque "road ports" and quiet secondary roads, often leading through pine forests and past natural springs. Situated within La Plana Alta, Benlloch provides a Mediterranean backdrop for outdoor activities. Best touring cycling routes around Benlloch The most popular touring cycling route is Cabanes and Via Augusta Loop, a 22.3 miles (35.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route incorporates secondary roads and access paths to picturesque local spots. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ermita de l'Adjutori – Coll de la Bandereta loop from Benlloc, a difficult 36.0 miles (58.0 km) path. This trail features demanding climbs that test endurance. Local touring cyclists also love the Les Coves de Vinromà – La Clapisa loop from Vilanova d'Alcolea, a 20.9 miles (33.8 km) trail leading through charming villages and quiet secondary roads, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Touring cycling around Benlloch is defined by diverse landscapes, "road ports," and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Benlloch are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1100 reviews. More than 5900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Benlloch's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Benlloch?
A: Benlloch offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 140 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, including 41 easy, 37 moderate, and 63 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Benlloch?
A: The terrain around Benlloch is quite varied, featuring roads full of contrasts. You'll find everything from flat sections ideal for speed to demanding climbs that test endurance. Routes often pass through picturesque "road ports," quiet secondary roads, and forest-type tracks leading through pine forests.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed ride?
A: Yes, Benlloch has 41 easy touring cycling routes. An excellent option for a relaxed ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31984198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de la Sal – Fortified Hermitage of Albalat loop from Hotel Marina Blanca&lt;/a&gt;. This 20 km route offers minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for a leisurely exploration.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Benlloch offers 63 difficult routes. While the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40334269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de l'Adjutori – Coll de la Bandereta loop from Benlloc&lt;/a&gt; is a popular demanding option, another challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938358349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabanes and Via Augusta Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This 35.9 km trail features significant elevation changes that will test your endurance.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Benlloch area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Benlloch are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31909595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of San Bartolomé – Well with water on the Camin Real loop from Vilanova d'Alcolea&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 19.1 km circular route that takes you through charming landscapes.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Benlloch's routes often pass by interesting landmarks and natural features. You can cycle past the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/219082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arch of Cabanes&lt;/a&gt;, a Roman arch dating from the 2nd to 3rd centuries. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/331570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortified Hermitage of Albalat&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3410303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Bandereta&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also lead through pine forests and past natural springs.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Benlloch?
A: The touring cycling routes in Benlloch are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 1100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of challenging climbs and scenic beauty, and the opportunity to explore charming villages and quiet secondary roads.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its picturesque "road ports" that provide beautiful landscapes. You can find routes that lead to viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7178624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcalà and its valley&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4027312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castell de Montornés&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning panoramas of the surrounding Mediterranean landscape and the Prat de Cabanes natural area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Benlloch?
A: Given Benlloch's Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling. The weather is typically mild, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter, making for comfortable riding conditions.

Q: Are there any specific natural features to look out for on the routes?
A: Yes, many routes will take you through beautiful natural settings. You can expect to cycle through extensive pine forests and discover natural springs. Specific locations like Subarra Fountain and Mas de Colau are integrated into some cycling loops, offering tranquil spots to enjoy the natural environment.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through charming villages?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Benlloch are designed to pass through charming villages and quiet secondary roads, allowing you to experience the authentic local culture. The historic "QUARTICO" Village, now part of Benlloch, is one such place that offers spectacular views and a glimpse into the region's heritage.
tips: From Cabanes to Vilanova d'Alcolea there are some remains of Roman buildings, in this case parts of the Via Augusta.
Roman arch, incomplete, since the upper body is missing above the arch. It dates between the 2nd and 3rd centuries, and it seems to be of a private honorific-funerary nature.

faqs.question: Is the Cabanes and Via Augusta Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this touring bicycle route is generally considered accessible for a wide range of cyclists, including beginners. It features very little elevation gain and predominantly follows rural roads, making it a relaxed ride rather than a challenging climb.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the route starts and ends near Benlloch and Cabanes. You'll likely find parking available in or near these villages, which serve as convenient access points for the loop.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on the loop is generally favorable for touring cyclists. It mainly consists of rural roads that closely follow the ancient Roman path, with some sections potentially incorporating quiet secondary roads. It's noted for having very little elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Cabanes and Via Augusta Loop?
faqs.answer: Given its Mediterranean location, spring and autumn are typically the best times to cycle this route. The weather is usually mild and pleasant, perfect for enjoying the scenery without the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: What historical sites or landmarks can I see on the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in history. You'll cycle along a segment of the ancient Via Augusta, encountering significant Roman vestiges like the Arch of Cabanes. The area also features thematic elements such as iron arches and giant bicycle sculptures, connecting you with its profound past.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in picturesque Mediterranean landscapes, cycling through extensive orange, almond, and olive groves. The route connects charming villages that have preserved their Roman legacy, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical ambiance.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Camino del Maestrazgo-Bajo Aragón (34%) and the Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana (34%). You'll also encounter smaller segments of paths like the Camí dels Romans and Camí de la Serra d'en Galceran.

faqs.question: Is the Cabanes and Via Augusta Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily uses rural roads, which are generally accessible, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near agricultural areas. Be mindful of local regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to cycle the Cabanes and Via Augusta Loop. It uses public roads and historical paths that are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route connects charming villages where you can find local amenities. These towns offer opportunities for a cultural and gastronomic experience, so you should be able to find cafes or small shops for refreshments and food.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: For a touring bicycle ride on this loop, pack essentials like water, snacks, a basic repair kit, and sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses). Comfortable cycling attire and appropriate footwear are also recommended. Don't forget your camera to capture the historical sites and scenic groves!
